 # Agent Notes: Kniffel Tracker
 
 Compact reference for OpenCode sessions working on this Django project.
 
 ## Project at a Glance
 
 - Single Django 6 app, SQLite by default, German-language UI (de-de / Europe/Berlin).
 - Apps: `users` (Player CRUD), `games` (Game, GamePlayer, ScoreEntry models + scoring logic).
 - Root URL redirects to `/games/`; admin lives at `/admin/`.
-- No JS framework; plain Django templates under `templates/` and per-app template dirs.
+- HTMX (loaded from CDN) for reactive scoring; otherwise plain Django templates.
 
 ## Environment
 
 - Target Python: **3.14.6** (declared in `.tool-versions`).
 - A local `.venv/` already exists and should be used.
 - Activate: `source .venv/bin/activate` or use `.venv/bin/python` directly.
 - Dependencies: `requirements.txt` pins `django==6.0.6`, `gunicorn`, and `whitenoise`.
 
 ## Common Commands
 
 Use `.venv/bin/python manage.py <cmd>` or activate the venv first:
 
 - Run dev server: `python manage.py runserver` → http://127.0.0.1:8000/
-- Run tests: `python manage.py test` (23 tests across `users/tests.py`, `games/tests.py`)
+- Run tests: `python manage.py test` (28 tests across `users/tests.py`, `games/tests.py`)
 - Run one app’s tests: `python manage.py test games` or `python manage.py test users`
 - Apply migrations: `python manage.py migrate`
 - Create admin user: `python manage.py createsuperuser`
 
 Make shortcuts (verify `GITHUB_USER` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `REMOTE_HOST` / `REMOTE_DIR`):
 
 - `make test` — run Django tests
 - `make build` — build Docker image
 - `make deploy REMOTE_HOST=... REMOTE_DIR=...` — SSH pull + recreate container
 
 ## Key Architecture Details
 
 - `ScoreEntry` uses a single TextChoices category set. Fixed lower-section categories (Full House, Small/Large Straight, Yahtzee) award constant points when state is `filled`; `stricken` always contributes 0.
 - `game_score` view POST handles both numeric categories and fixed-category state dropdowns. Field names in HTML must be:
   - Numeric: `score_<gameplayer_id>_<category>`
   - Fixed state: `state_<gameplayer_id>_<category>` (values: `empty`, `filled`, `stricken`)
+- `game_score_partial` is an HTMX endpoint that saves a single changed entry on `change` and returns updated total rows (`#upper-totals` and `#lower-totals` via `hx-swap-oob`).
 - `GamePlayer.total_score` is computed on the fly from `ScoreEntry` rows; there is no denormalized total field.
 - Creating/editing/deleting players and games is restricted to authenticated users via `@login_required`; lists and detail/result/leaderboard views remain public.
 - Leaderboard counts wins by checking whether a player ranked first in each completed game.
 
 ## Docker / Production Notes
 
 - `docker compose up -d --build` uses SQLite persisted in named volume `kniffel_data` mounted at `/app/data`.
 - The container entrypoint (`docker-entrypoint.sh`) runs `collectstatic --noinput` and `migrate --noinput` on every start, then launches Gunicorn.
 - Override in `docker-compose.yml` before real deployment: `SECRET_KEY`, `DEBUG=False`, `ALLOWED_HOSTS`.
 - `DATABASE_PATH` env var overrides the SQLite location; Docker sets it to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
 - Production static files are served by WhiteNoise; `STATIC_ROOT` is `BASE_DIR / "staticfiles"`.
 - `Makefile` defaults to `GITHUB_USER=jaadrian`; override via env or argument.
 
 ## Fly.io Deployment
 
 The repo includes a `fly.toml` that builds the image from the repo's `Dockerfile` and deploys it with a persistent Fly Volume for the SQLite database.
 
 ### First-time setup
 
 ```bash
 fly apps create yahtzee
 fly volumes create kniffel_data --region ams --size 1
 fly secrets set SECRET_KEY="<generate-with-secrets.token_urlsafe(50)>" ALLOWED_HOSTS="yahtzee.fly.dev"
 fly deploy
 ```
 
 ### Required env vars
 
 - `SECRET_KEY` — Django secret key (required, never use the dev default).
 - `ALLOWED_HOSTS` — Fly app hostname(s), comma-separated; used by Django host validation.
 - `DATABASE_PATH` — already set in `fly.toml` to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
 - `DEBUG` — set to `"False"` in `fly.toml`.
 - `SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER` and `CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` are configured in `settings.py` so HTTPS/CSRF work behind Fly's proxy.
 
 ### fly.toml notes
 
 - Build source: the repo's `Dockerfile` (managed by Fly on deploy).
 - Volume `kniffel_data` is mounted at `/app/data` so SQLite survives redeploys.
 - `auto_stop_machines = "stop"` and `min_machines_running = 0` allow the free-scale Machine to stop when idle. If you want it always on, set `min_machines_running = 1` and `auto_stop_machines = "off"`.
 - Because SQLite cannot handle multiple writers safely, keep the app scaled to **a single Machine** (`fly scale count 1`).
 - VM size defaults to `shared-cpu-1x` / 256 MB, which is enough for this app.
 
 ### Updating the app
 
 Deploy from the local repo (no Git push required):
 
 ```bash
 fly deploy --ha=false
 ```
 
 `--ha=false` prevents Fly from launching a second Machine, which would break the single-volume SQLite setup.
 
 ### Creating an admin user on Fly
 
 ```bash
 fly ssh console
 python /app/manage.py createsuperuser
 ```
 
 ## Things That Are Easy to Miss
 
 - `DEBUG` defaults to `True` and `SECRET_KEY` falls back to a hardcoded dev key when env vars are absent. Do not rely on these defaults for any deployment.
 - `ALLOWED_HOSTS` is empty in debug mode and `localhost/127.0.0.1` in production mode if not explicitly set.
 - Player names are unique case-insensitively (`Player.name` is unique at DB level; the view normalizes duplicates via lowercase comparison).
 - The `.gitignore` explicitly ignores `AGENTS.md`; if you edit this file, stage it manually with `git add -f AGENTS.md`.

 from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
 from django.test import TestCase
 from django.urls import reverse
 
 from users.models import Player
 
 from .models import Game, GamePlayer, ScoreEntry
 
 
 class ScoreEntryModelTests(TestCase):
     def test_fixed_category_effective_value(self):
         gp = self._create_game_player()
         entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
             game_player=gp,
             category=ScoreEntry.Category.FULL_HOUSE,
             state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED,
         )
         self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 25)
         self.assertTrue(entry.is_filled)
         self.assertFalse(entry.is_stricken)
 
     def test_stricken_entry_contributes_zero(self):
         gp = self._create_game_player()
         entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
             game_player=gp,
             category=ScoreEntry.Category.YAHTZEE,
             state=ScoreEntry.State.STRICKEN,
         )
         self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 0)
         self.assertTrue(entry.is_stricken)
 
     def test_upper_section_entry_value(self):
         gp = self._create_game_player()
         entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
             game_player=gp,
             category=ScoreEntry.Category.ONES,
             value=3,
             state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED,
         )
         self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 3)
 
     def _create_game_player(self):
         game = Game.objects.create()
         player = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
         return GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=player)
 
 
 class GamePlayerScoreTests(TestCase):
     def setUp(self):
         self.game = Game.objects.create()
         self.alice = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
         self.gp_alice = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=self.game, player=self.alice)
 
     def test_upper_sum(self):
         self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.ONES, 3)
         self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.TWOS, 6)
         self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.THREES, 9)

 def _ensure_score_entries(game_player):
     """Create missing ScoreEntry rows for all editable categories."""
     existing = set(game_player.scores.values_list("category", flat=True))
     missing = [cat for cat in SCORE_SHEET_ORDER if cat not in existing]
     ScoreEntry.objects.bulk_create(
         [ScoreEntry(game_player=game_player, category=cat) for cat in missing]
     )
 
 
 def _parse_score_entry(entry, raw_value, raw_state):
     """Update entry value/state from form POST data.
 
     Fixed lower-section categories (Full House, straights, Yahtzee) use a state
     dropdown: empty, filled (awards fixed points) or stricken (0 points).
 
     All numeric categories (upper section and free lower-section categories)
     simply use a numeric input; 0 is a normal score, empty means not yet entered.
     """
     if entry.category in ScoreEntry.FIXED_POINTS:
         entry.value = None
         if raw_state == "filled":
             entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.FILLED
         elif raw_state == "stricken":
             entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.STRICKEN
         else:
             entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.EMPTY
         return True
 
     # Numeric categories: just a number, empty = not entered.
     if raw_value == "":
         entry.value = None
         entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.EMPTY
         return True
 
     try:
         entry.value = int(raw_value)
         entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.FILLED
         return True
     except ValueError:
         return False

 def game_finish(request, pk):
     game = get_object_or_404(Game, pk=pk)
     if request.method == "POST":
         with transaction.atomic():
             game.is_complete = True
             game.finished_at = timezone.now()
             game.save()
         messages.success(request, f"Spiel #{game.id} wurde abgeschlossen.")
         return HttpResponseRedirect(reverse("game_results", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}))
     return render(request, "games/game_finish.html", {"game": game})

     return render(request, "games/game_delete.html", {"game": game})
 
 
 def game_results(request, pk):

 def leaderboard(request):
     """Eternal leaderboard: players ranked by completed-game wins."""
     players = Player.objects.order_by("name")
     stats = []
     for player in players:
         wins = 0
         games_played = player.game_entries.filter(game__is_complete=True).count()
-        for gp in player.game_entries.filter(game__is_complete=True).prefetch_related("game__game_players"):
+        for gp in player.game_entries.filter(game__is_complete=True).prefetch_related(
+            "game__game_players"
+        ):
             ranked = gp.game.ranked_players()
             if ranked and ranked[0].pk == gp.pk:
                 wins += 1
         stats.append({"player": player, "wins": wins, "games_played": games_played})
 
     stats.sort(key=lambda s: (-s["wins"], -s["games_played"], s["player"].name))
     return render(request, "games/leaderboard.html", {"stats": stats})

i'd be in favor of adding HTMX if this is exactly for that purpose. that would make changes in the future easier, i guess.
if you have strong feelings for vanilla, we do that instead.

questions:
- let's do the "lose focus" way. else the UX could be strange for the user.

please proceed

OK, HTMX it is. It is exactly built for this pattern and keeps the HTML declarative.

Plan:
1. Add HTMX via CDN to the base template.
2. Mark each score input/select with `hx-post` to a new `game_score_partial` endpoint, `hx-trigger="change"`, and target the total/bonus rows for that player.
3. Add a new view `game_score_partial(request, pk)` that saves one changed entry and returns only the sum rows HTML (or the whole table body to keep it simple).

Let me implement that now.

display.text:
from django.contrib import messages
from django.contrib.auth.decorators import login_required
from django.db import transaction
from django.http import HttpResponseRedirect
from django.shortcuts import get_object_or_404, render
from django.urls import reverse
from django.utils import timezone

from users.models import Player

from .models import Game, GamePlayer, ScoreEntry


# Display order matching a real German Yahtzee score sheet.
SCORE_SHEET_ORDER = [
    ScoreEntry.Category.ONES,
    ScoreEntry.Category.TWOS,
    ScoreEntry.Category.THREES,
    ScoreEntry.Category.FOURS,
    ScoreEntry.Category.FIVES,
    ScoreEntry.Category.SIXES,
    ScoreEntry.Category.THREE_OF_A_KIND,
    ScoreEntry.Category.FOUR_OF_A_KIND,
    ScoreEntry.Category.FULL_HOUSE,
    ScoreEntry.Category.SMALL_STRAIGHT,
    ScoreEntry.Category.LARGE_STRAIGHT,
    ScoreEntry.Category.YAHTZEE,
    ScoreEntry.Category.CHANCE,
]


def game_list(request):
    active_games = Game.objects.filter(is_complete=False).order_by("-created_at")
    finished_games = Game.objects.filter(is_complete=True).order_by("-finished_at")
    return render(
        request,
        "games/game_list.html",
        {"active_games": active_games, "finished_games": finished_games},
    )


@login_required
def game_create(request):
    if request.method == "POST":
        game = Game.objects.create()
        messages.success(request, f"Spiel #{game.id} wurde gestartet.")
        return HttpResponseRedirect(game.get_absolute_url())
    return render(request, "games/game_create.html")

+
+@login_required
+def game_score_partial(request, pk):
+    """Save one score entry from an HTMX request and return updated totals."""
+    game = get_object_or_404(Game, pk=pk)
+    if game.is_complete:
+        return HttpResponseBadRequest(
+            "Abgeschlossene Spiele können nicht bearbeitet werden."
+        )
+
+    gp = get_object_or_404(GamePlayer, pk=request.POST.get("game_player_id"), game=game)
+    category = request.POST.get("category")
+    if category not in {cat.value for cat in SCORE_SHEET_ORDER}:
+        return HttpResponseBadRequest("Ungültige Kategorie.")
+
+    _ensure_score_entries(gp)
+    entry = gp.scores.get(category=category)
+
+    value_field = f"score_{gp.pk}_{category}"
+    state_field = f"state_{gp.pk}_{category}"
+    raw_value = request.POST.get(value_field, "").strip()
+    raw_state = request.POST.get(state_field, "").strip()
+
+    if not _parse_score_entry(entry, raw_value, raw_state):
+        return HttpResponseBadRequest(
+            f"Ungültiger Wert bei {gp.player.name} / {ScoreEntry.Category(category).label}."
+        )
+
+    entry.save(update_fields=["value", "state"])
+
+    return render(
+        request,
+        "games/game_score_partial.html",
+        {
+            "game": game,
+            "players": list(
+                game.game_players.select_related("player").prefetch_related("scores")
+            ),
+            "fixed_points": ScoreEntry.FIXED_POINTS,
+        },
+    )
+
     # Re-fetch after ensuring rows exist.
     players = list(
         game.game_players.select_related("player").prefetch_related("scores")
     )

parts.text:
Now I need to create the partial template `games/game_score_partial.html`. It should return the entire table body so HTMX can swap it in place. The target will be the `<tbody>` of the score sheet. Actually, better: target the entire table or tbody. Let me design it.

In `game_score.html`:
- Wrap the `<tbody>` with an `id="score-body"`
- Each input/select gets `hx-post="{% url 'game_score_partial' game.pk %}" hx-target="#score-body" hx-trigger="change" hx-swap="innerHTML" hx-vals='{"game_player_id": "{{ gp.pk }}", "category": "{{ category.value }}"}' hx-include="this"` ... actually, HTMX will include the triggering element's value automatically, but we need `game_player_id` and `category` included. We can use `hx-vals` to send those.

Wait, `hx-vals` sends them as part of the request. The input name will also be sent. Good.

But the select/input itself is the target. For a number input, when it triggers, HTMX will send its value. For a select, same.

However, the `#score-body` swap will replace the entire tbody, which means focus will be lost. Better to target a smaller element, like the specific player's total cells, but that complicates things.

Alternative: Use `hx-swap-oob` in the partial template to update the sum rows out-of-band. Or target the whole table body and re-focus after swap.

Simpler: target a `<tr id="totals-row">` or use `hx-swap="outerHTML"` on the input row? Hmm.

Actually, the best HTMX approach for a table is to return the whole table body and let HTMX swap it. To preserve focus, we can use `hx-preserve` on the inputs, but the sum rows need to update.

Wait, simpler design: Keep the table static, but have a separate endpoint return just the totals. Use HTMX to target only the totals rows. The user changes a value -> HTMX posts to partial endpoint -> returns updated total rows -> swaps them in.

But totals depend on all players, so we'd need to update all totals. We can have a `tbody` or `tfoot` that contains the sum rows (upper sum, bonus, lower sum, total), and target that. Each input when changed posts to the endpoint and returns the new totals for all players.

Let me restructure `game_score.html`:
- The data rows stay static and are not swapped.
- A separate `tfoot` (or a `<tbody id="score-totals">`) contains the sum rows and is the `hx-target`.
- Each input/select has `hx-post="{% url 'game_score_partial' game.pk %}" hx-target="#score-totals" hx-trigger="change" hx-vals='{"game_player_id": "{{ gp.pk }}", "category": "{{ category.value }}"}'`.
- The partial template renders only the totals rows.

This preserves input focus and is clean.

Let me write the partial template first. It needs to render:
- Sum Oben row
- Bonus row
- Lower sum row
- Total row

But these need to be in a table section. I'll use a `<tbody id="score-totals">`.

Actually, the upper sum and bonus rows appear after sixes in the existing table, not at the end. To keep structure, I can:
- Put the sum rows (upper, bonus, lower, total) all in a separate `tbody` at the end, OR
- Use HTMX to swap multiple OOB targets.

I think the cleanest is to have the entire `tbody` be the target, but preserve focus via `hx-preserve` on the input that's currently focused. Actually, `hx-preserve` works by id: the old element with the same id is kept. So if each input has a unique id, it will be preserved during swap. But we also need to update its value from the server? No, the user just typed it, so keeping it is fine. The server response will have the updated value from DB, but preserving means the DOM element stays with the user's value.

Hmm, but with `hx-preserve`, the input stays, but its name/attributes are preserved. That's fine.

Alternative: Return the whole tbody and use `hx-select` to update only the sum rows? No, that's complicated.

Let me go with the `tfoot`/`tbody` approach for totals. The data rows stay in a `tbody`, totals in another `tbody id="score-totals"`. On change, only the totals tbody swaps.

But wait, the bonus is currently shown after sixes. If I move it to the end in a separate tbody, the visual order changes. That's actually OK and maybe clearer. But let me preserve the current visual order.

Actually, I can have two total targets:
1. `#upper-totals` after sixes
2. `#lower-totals` at the bottom

The partial endpoint returns both as out-of-band swaps. This is clean.

Let me design:
- After sixes row, wrap the upper sum + bonus rows in `<tbody id="upper-totals">`.
- At the bottom, wrap lower sum + total rows in `<tbody id="lower-totals">`.
- The partial template renders both `tbody`s with `hx-swap-oob="true"`.

Then inputs target either `#upper-totals`? No, with OOB we can target just the trigger element's closest... Actually, HTMX will swap the main target, and OOB elements will also swap. The main target can be `#score-totals` (a hidden div or the lower totals).

Simpler: set `hx-target="#upper-totals"` and include both `tbody`s in the response with `hx-swap-oob="true"`. The main swap will update `#upper-totals`, and the OOB will update `#lower-totals`.

Wait, actually if we set `hx-target="#upper-totals"` and the response contains:
```html
<tbody id="upper-totals" hx-swap-oob="true">...</tbody>
<tbody id="lower-totals" hx-swap-oob="true">...</tbody>
```

HTMX will swap the main target (#upper-totals) and also swap the OOB targets. This works.

Let me create the partial template to render both `tbody`s.

Actually, even simpler: make the partial return the entire table body with both totals sections. Set the main target to the existing table body but use `hx-preserve`? Or just return the totals sections.

I think the OOB approach is best. Let me implement.

First, update `game_score.html` to have the totals sections with ids, and add HTMX attributes to inputs/selects.

Then create `game_score_partial.html` to render the totals sections.

Let me also consider CSRF: HTMX automatically reads the CSRF token from the page if `htmx:configRequest` is set up, or we can include it via `hx-headers` or `hx-vals` with `{% csrf_token %}`. Django's CSRF is needed for POST.

Easiest: include the CSRF token in `hx-vals`:
`hx-vals='{"csrfmiddlewaretoken": "{{ csrf_token }}", "game_player_id": ...}'`

But `{{ csrf_token }}` is the token string. We need the actual value. In templates, `{% csrf_token %}` renders the hidden input. To get just the value, we can use `{{ csrf_token }}` in a template variable context if we add it to context, or simpler: put `{% csrf_token %}` inside the form and let HTMX include it automatically because HTMX includes all inputs in the nearest form by default when using `hx-post` on a form child? Actually, HTMX includes the element's value and other inputs if `hx-include` is set. By default, for an input with `hx-post`, it includes only that input.

But if the input is inside a `<form>`, HTMX will include the nearest form's values when the element has `hx-post`? Let me recall: HTMX uses `getInputValues` and includes the element and its closest form? Actually, HTMX includes the element itself and any elements specified by `hx-include`. If the element is an input, it includes its value. It does NOT automatically include the whole form unless `hx-post` is on the form or `hx-include` is used.

So we need to include CSRF. Options:
1. Add `hx-headers='{"X-CSRFToken": "{{ csrf_token }}"}'` — but Django expects `csrfmiddlewaretoken` in POST data by default, or `X-CSRFToken` header.
2. Add a hidden `{% csrf_token %}` and use `hx-include="closest form"` on each input. But that includes all inputs on every change, which is not what we want (we want single save).
3. Use `hx-vals='{"csrfmiddlewaretoken": "{{ csrf_token }}"}'` — this requires the actual token value in the attribute. Since `{{ csrf_token }}` in the template just returns the string value (it's a context processor variable), we can use it: `hx-vals='{"csrfmiddlewaretoken": "{{ csrf_token }}", ...}'`.

Wait, does `{{ csrf_token }}` output the raw token string or an input? In Django templates, `{{ csrf_token }}` is the CSRF token string. `{% csrf_token %}` outputs the hidden input. So we can use `{{ csrf_token }}`.

But the CSRF token might contain characters that break the JSON in the attribute. It should be safe, but to be sure, we can use `hx-headers='{"X-CSRFToken": "{{ csrf_token }}"}'` and ensure Django's CSRF middleware accepts the header. Django's CSRF middleware does accept `X-CSRFToken` header by default.

Actually, the cleanest HTMX-Django integration is to listen for `htmx:configRequest` and add the CSRF token. But that requires a small JS snippet.

Given the scope, I'll add a tiny JS snippet in base.html that reads the CSRF token from the `csrfmiddlewaretoken` input (or meta) and sets it on HTMX requests. Then all HTMX requests are CSRF-safe.

Actually, simpler: just add `hx-vals='{"csrfmiddlewaretoken": "{{ csrf_token }}"}'`. Let me check if `{{ csrf_token }}` works. In Django, yes, `csrf_token` context variable is available and is the token string.

But wait, the `csrf_token` variable is only available if `django.template.context_processors.csrf` is enabled. It's enabled by default. Good.

However, the token string may need escaping inside the JSON attribute. Using a meta tag is cleaner. Let me add a `<meta name="csrf-token" content="{{ csrf_token }}">` in base.html and a small script that configures HTMX to send it as `X-CSRFToken`.

This is robust and reusable.

Let me do that. Add to base.html:
```html
<meta name="csrf-token" content="{{ csrf_token }}">
<script>
  document.body.addEventListener('htmx:configRequest', function(evt) {
    evt.detail.headers['X-CSRFToken'] = document.querySelector('meta[name="csrf-token"]').getAttribute('content');
  });
</script>
```

This needs to be after the HTMX script and after body load? Actually, `htmx:configRequest` events are dispatched from the element, so we can listen on document.body. The script should be after the HTMX script tag. I'll put both in the `<head>`, with the config script after the HTMX script.

Now let me implement the template changes.</think>

98: def _ensure_score_entries(game_player):
99:     """Create missing ScoreEntry rows for all editable categories."""
100:     existing = set(game_player.scores.values_list("category", flat=True))
101:     missing = [cat for cat in SCORE_SHEET_ORDER if cat not in existing]
102:     ScoreEntry.objects.bulk_create(
103:         [ScoreEntry(game_player=game_player, category=cat) for cat in missing]
104:     )
105: 
106: 
107: def _parse_score_entry(entry, raw_value, raw_state):
108:     """Update entry value/state from form POST data.
109: 
110:     Fixed lower-section categories (Full House, straights, Yahtzee) use a state
111:     dropdown: empty, filled (awards fixed points) or stricken (0 points).
112: 
113:     All numeric categories (upper section and free lower-section categories)
114:     simply use a numeric input; 0 is a normal score, empty means not yet entered.
115:     """
116:     if entry.category in ScoreEntry.FIXED_POINTS:
117:         entry.value = None
118:         if raw_state == "filled":
119:             entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.FILLED
120:         elif raw_state == "stricken":
121:             entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.STRICKEN
122:         else:
123:             entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.EMPTY
124:         return True
125: 
126:     # Numeric categories: just a number, empty = not entered.
127:     if raw_value == "":
128:         entry.value = None
129:         entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.EMPTY
130:         return True
131: 
132:     try:
133:         entry.value = int(raw_value)
134:         entry.state = ScoreEntry.State.FILLED
135:         return True
136:     except ValueError:
137:         return False
138:

146:         )

149:     players = list(

151:     )
152:     if not players:
153:         messages.error(request, "Füge zuerst Spieler zum Spiel hinzu.")
154:         return HttpResponseRedirect(game.get_absolute_url())
155: 
156:     for gp in players:
157:         _ensure_score_entries(gp)
158:

162:     )

165:         entries_to_create = []

174:

184:                     )

    def test_cannot_add_player_to_finished_game(self):
        game = Game.objects.create(is_complete=True)
        response = self.client.get(reverse("game_add_player", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(game.game_players.count(), 0)

    def test_score_sheet_saves_numeric_values(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        gp = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=self.alice)
        url = reverse("game_score", kwargs={"pk": game.pk})
        response = self.client.get(url)
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)

        response = self.client.post(
            url,
            {
                f"score_{gp.pk}_ones": "5",
                f"score_{gp.pk}_chance": "22",
            },
        )
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        gp.refresh_from_db()
        self.assertEqual(gp.total_score, 27)

    def test_score_sheet_saves_fixed_category_states(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        gp = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=self.alice)
        url = reverse("game_score", kwargs={"pk": game.pk})
        response = self.client.post(
            url,
            {
                f"state_{gp.pk}_full_house": "filled",
                f"state_{gp.pk}_yahtzee": "stricken",
            },
        )
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        gp.refresh_from_db()
        self.assertEqual(gp.lower_sum, 25)

    def test_numeric_zero_is_valid_score(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        gp = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=self.alice)
        url = reverse("game_score", kwargs={"pk": game.pk})
        response = self.client.post(
            url,
            {f"score_{gp.pk}_three_of_a_kind": "0"},
        )
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        entry = gp.scores.get(category=ScoreEntry.Category.THREE_OF_A_KIND)
        self.assertTrue(entry.is_filled)
        self.assertEqual(entry.value, 0)
        self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 0)

    def test_finish_game_redirects_to_results(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        response = self.client.post(reverse("game_finish", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertTrue(response.url.endswith(reverse("game_results", kwargs={"pk": game.pk})))
        game.refresh_from_db()
        self.assertTrue(game.is_complete)

    def test_delete_game(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        response = self.client.post(reverse("game_delete", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertFalse(Game.objects.filter(pk=game.pk).exists())

    def test_leaderboard_counts_wins(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        gp_alice = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=self.alice)
        gp_bob = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=self.bob)
        ScoreEntry.objects.update_or_create(
            game_player=gp_alice,
            category=ScoreEntry.Category.ONES,
            defaults={"value": 5, "state": ScoreEntry.State.FILLED},
        )
        ScoreEntry.objects.update_or_create(
            game_player=gp_bob,
            category=ScoreEntry.Category.ONES,
            defaults={"value": 3, "state": ScoreEntry.State.FILLED},
        )
        self.client.post(reverse("game_finish", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}))

        response = self.client.get(reverse("leaderboard"))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)
        self.assertContains(response, "Alice")
        self.assertContains(response, "Bob")

from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
from django.test import TestCase
from django.urls import reverse

from users.models import Player

from .models import Game, GamePlayer, ScoreEntry


class ScoreEntryModelTests(TestCase):
    def test_fixed_category_effective_value(self):
        gp = self._create_game_player()
        entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
            game_player=gp,
            category=ScoreEntry.Category.FULL_HOUSE,
            state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED,
        )
        self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 25)
        self.assertTrue(entry.is_filled)
        self.assertFalse(entry.is_stricken)

    def test_stricken_entry_contributes_zero(self):
        gp = self._create_game_player()
        entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
            game_player=gp,
            category=ScoreEntry.Category.YAHTZEE,
            state=ScoreEntry.State.STRICKEN,
        )
        self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 0)
        self.assertTrue(entry.is_stricken)

    def test_upper_section_entry_value(self):
        gp = self._create_game_player()
        entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
            game_player=gp,
            category=ScoreEntry.Category.ONES,
            value=3,
            state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED,
        )
        self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 3)

    def _create_game_player(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        player = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
        return GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=player)


class GamePlayerScoreTests(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.game = Game.objects.create()
        self.alice = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
        self.gp_alice = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=self.game, player=self.alice)

    def test_upper_sum(self):
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.ONES, 3)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.TWOS, 6)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.THREES, 9)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FOURS, 12)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FIVES, 15)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.SIXES, 18)

    def test_bonus_at_63(self):
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.ONES, 3)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.TWOS, 6)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.THREES, 9)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FOURS, 12)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FIVES, 15)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.SIXES, 18)
        self.assertEqual(self.gp_alice.bonus, 35)

    def test_no_bonus_below_63(self):
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.ONES, 1)
        self.assertEqual(self.gp_alice.bonus, 0)

    def test_lower_sum_with_fixed_and_free_categories(self):
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FULL_HOUSE, state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.SMALL_STRAIGHT, state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.YAHTZEE, state=ScoreEntry.State.STRICKEN)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.THREE_OF_A_KIND, value=18)
        self.assertEqual(self.gp_alice.lower_sum, 25 + 30 + 0 + 18)

    def test_total_score(self):
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.ONES, 3)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.TWOS, 6)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.THREES, 9)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FOURS, 12)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FIVES, 15)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.SIXES, 18)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FULL_HOUSE, state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED)
        self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.CHANCE, value=22)
        expected = 63 + 35 + 25 + 22
        self.assertEqual(self.gp_alice.total_score, expected)

    def _set(self, category, value=None, state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED):
        ScoreEntry.objects.update_or_create(
            game_player=self.gp_alice,
            category=category,
            defaults={"value": value, "state": state},
        )

        game = Game.objects.create()

            reverse("game_add_player", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}),
            {"players": [self.alice.pk, self.bob.pk]},
        )
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(game.game_players.count(), 2)

31: - `make test` — run Django tests

33: - `make deploy REMOTE_HOST=... REMOTE_DIR=...` — SSH pull + recreate container
34: 
35: ## Key Architecture Details
36: 
37: - `ScoreEntry` uses a single TextChoices category set. Fixed lower-section categories (Full House, Small/Large Straight, Yahtzee) award constant points when state is `filled`; `stricken` always contributes 0.
38: - `game_score` view POST handles both numeric categories and fixed-category state dropdowns. Field names in HTML must be:
39:   - Numeric: `score_<gameplayer_id>_<category>`

41: - `GamePlayer.total_score` is computed on the fly from `ScoreEntry` rows; there is no denormalized total field.
42: - Creating/editing/deleting players and games is restricted to authenticated users via `@login_required`; lists and detail/result/leaderboard views remain public.
43: - Leaderboard counts wins by checking whether a player ranked first in each completed game.
44: 
45: ## Docker / Production Notes
46: 
47: - `docker compose up -d --build` uses SQLite persisted in named volume `kniffel_data` mounted at `/app/data`.
48: - The container entrypoint (`docker-entrypoint.sh`) runs `collectstatic --noinput` and `migrate --noinput` on every start, then launches Gunicorn.
49: - Override in `docker-compose.yml` before real deployment: `SECRET_KEY`, `DEBUG=False`, `ALLOWED_HOSTS`.
50: - `DATABASE_PATH` env var overrides the SQLite location; Docker sets it to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
51: - Production static files are served by WhiteNoise; `STATIC_ROOT` is `BASE_DIR / "staticfiles"`.
52: - `Makefile` defaults to `GITHUB_USER=jaadrian`; override via env or argument.
53: 
54: ## Fly.io Deployment
55: 
56: The repo includes a `fly.toml` that builds the image from the repo's `Dockerfile` and deploys it with a persistent Fly Volume for the SQLite database.
57: 
58: ### First-time setup
59: 
60: ```bash
61: fly apps create yahtzee
62: fly volumes create kniffel_data --region ams --size 1
63: fly secrets set SECRET_KEY="<generate-with-secrets.token_urlsafe(50)>" ALLOWED_HOSTS="yahtzee.fly.dev"
64: fly deploy
65: ```
66: 
67: ### Required env vars
68:

70: - `ALLOWED_HOSTS` — Fly app hostname(s), comma-separated; used by Django host validation.
71: - `DATABASE_PATH` — already set in `fly.toml` to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
72: - `DEBUG` — set to `"False"` in `fly.toml`.
73: - `SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER` and `CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` are configured in `settings.py` so HTTPS/CSRF work behind Fly's proxy.
74: 
75: ### fly.toml notes
76: 
77: - Build source: the repo's `Dockerfile` (managed by Fly on deploy).
78: - Volume `kniffel_data` is mounted at `/app/data` so SQLite survives redeploys.
79: - `auto_stop_machines = "stop"` and `min_machines_running = 0` allow the free-scale Machine to stop when idle. If you want it always on, set `min_machines_running = 1` and `auto_stop_machines = "off"`.
80: - Because SQLite cannot handle multiple writers safely, keep the app scaled to **a single Machine** (`fly scale count 1`).
81: - VM size defaults to `shared-cpu-1x` / 256 MB, which is enough for this app.
82:

92: 
93: ### Creating an admin user on Fly

98: ```
99: 
100: ## Things That Are Easy to Miss
101: 
102: - `DEBUG` defaults to `True` and `SECRET_KEY` falls back to a hardcoded dev key when env vars are absent. Do not rely on these defaults for any deployment.
103: - `ALLOWED_HOSTS` is empty in debug mode and `localhost/127.0.0.1` in production mode if not explicitly set.
104: - Player names are unique case-insensitively (`Player.name` is unique at DB level; the view normalizes duplicates via lowercase comparison).

display.text:
# Agent Notes: Kniffel Tracker

Compact reference for OpenCode sessions working on this Django project.

## Project at a Glance

- Single Django 6 app, SQLite by default, German-language UI (de-de / Europe/Berlin).
- Apps: `users` (Player CRUD), `games` (Game, GamePlayer, ScoreEntry models + scoring logic).
- Root URL redirects to `/games/`; admin lives at `/admin/`.
- No JS framework; plain Django templates under `templates/` and per-app template dirs.

## Environment

- Target Python: **3.14.6** (declared in `.tool-versions`).
- A local `.venv/` already exists and should be used.
- Activate: `source .venv/bin/activate` or use `.venv/bin/python` directly.
- Dependencies: `requirements.txt` pins `django==6.0.6`, `gunicorn`, and `whitenoise`.

## Common Commands

Use `.venv/bin/python manage.py <cmd>` or activate the venv first:

- Run dev server: `python manage.py runserver` → http://127.0.0.1:8000/
- Run tests: `python manage.py test` (23 tests across `users/tests.py`, `games/tests.py`)
- Run one app’s tests: `python manage.py test games` or `python manage.py test users`
- Apply migrations: `python manage.py migrate`
- Create admin user: `python manage.py createsuperuser`

Make shortcuts (verify `GITHUB_USER` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `REMOTE_HOST` / `REMOTE_DIR`):

- `make test` — run Django tests
- `make build` — build Docker image
- `make deploy REMOTE_HOST=... REMOTE_DIR=...` — SSH pull + recreate container

## Key Architecture Details

- `ScoreEntry` uses a single TextChoices category set. Fixed lower-section categories (Full House, Small/Large Straight, Yahtzee) award constant points when state is `filled`; `stricken` always contributes 0.
- `game_score` view POST handles both numeric categories and fixed-category state dropdowns. Field names in HTML must be:
  - Numeric: `score_<gameplayer_id>_<category>`
  - Fixed state: `state_<gameplayer_id>_<category>` (values: `empty`, `filled`, `stricken`)
- `GamePlayer.total_score` is computed on the fly from `ScoreEntry` rows; there is no denormalized total field.
- Creating/editing/deleting players and games is restricted to authenticated users via `@login_required`; lists and detail/result/leaderboard views remain public.
- Leaderboard counts wins by checking whether a player ranked first in each completed game.

## Docker / Production Notes

- `docker compose up -d --build` uses SQLite persisted in named volume `kniffel_data` mounted at `/app/data`.
- The container entrypoint (`docker-entrypoint.sh`) runs `collectstatic --noinput` and `migrate --noinput` on every start, then launches Gunicorn.
- Override in `docker-compose.yml` before real deployment: `SECRET_KEY`, `DEBUG=False`, `ALLOWED_HOSTS`.
- `DATABASE_PATH` env var overrides the SQLite location; Docker sets it to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
- Production static files are served by WhiteNoise; `STATIC_ROOT` is `BASE_DIR / "staticfiles"`.
- `Makefile` defaults to `GITHUB_USER=jaadrian`; override via env or argument.

## Fly.io Deployment

The repo includes a `fly.toml` that builds the image from the repo's `Dockerfile` and deploys it with a persistent Fly Volume for the SQLite database.

### First-time setup

```bash
fly apps create yahtzee
fly volumes create kniffel_data --region ams --size 1
fly secrets set SECRET_KEY="<generate-with-secrets.token_urlsafe(50)>" ALLOWED_HOSTS="yahtzee.fly.dev"
fly deploy
```

### Required env vars

- `SECRET_KEY` — Django secret key (required, never use the dev default).
- `ALLOWED_HOSTS` — Fly app hostname(s), comma-separated; used by Django host validation.
- `DATABASE_PATH` — already set in `fly.toml` to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
- `DEBUG` — set to `"False"` in `fly.toml`.
- `SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER` and `CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` are configured in `settings.py` so HTTPS/CSRF work behind Fly's proxy.

### fly.toml notes

- Build source: the repo's `Dockerfile` (managed by Fly on deploy).
- Volume `kniffel_data` is mounted at `/app/data` so SQLite survives redeploys.
- `auto_stop_machines = "stop"` and `min_machines_running = 0` allow the free-scale Machine to stop when idle. If you want it always on, set `min_machines_running = 1` and `auto_stop_machines = "off"`.
- Because SQLite cannot handle multiple writers safely, keep the app scaled to **a single Machine** (`fly scale count 1`).
- VM size defaults to `shared-cpu-1x` / 256 MB, which is enough for this app.

### Updating the app

Deploy from the local repo (no Git push required):

```bash
fly deploy --ha=false
```

`--ha=false` prevents Fly from launching a second Machine, which would break the single-volume SQLite setup.

### Creating an admin user on Fly

```bash
fly ssh console
python /app/manage.py createsuperuser
```

## Things That Are Easy to Miss

- `DEBUG` defaults to `True` and `SECRET_KEY` falls back to a hardcoded dev key when env vars are absent. Do not rely on these defaults for any deployment.
- `ALLOWED_HOSTS` is empty in debug mode and `localhost/127.0.0.1` in production mode if not explicitly set.
- Player names are unique case-insensitively (`Player.name` is unique at DB level; the view normalizes duplicates via lowercase comparison).
- The `.gitignore` explicitly ignores `AGENTS.md`; if you edit this file, stage it manually with `git add -f AGENTS.md`.

 - `GamePlayer.total_score` is computed on the fly from `ScoreEntry` rows; there is no denormalized total field.
 - Creating/editing/deleting players and games is restricted to authenticated users via `@login_required`; lists and detail/result/leaderboard views remain public.
 - Leaderboard counts wins by checking whether a player ranked first in each completed game.

parts.text:
Done. I implemented reactive scoring with HTMX:

- **Backend**: Added `game_score_partial` view that saves a single entry on `change` and returns updated totals.
- **Templates**: Split `game_score.html` into upper/lower sections, added HTMX attributes to inputs/selects, created `_score_totals_upper.html`, `_score_totals_lower.html`, and `game_score_partial.html` with `hx-swap-oob`.
- **CSRF**: Added HTMX config in `base.html` to send Django's CSRF token via `X-CSRFToken`.
- **HTMX**: Loaded from CDN with SRI hash.
- **Tests**: Added 5 tests for the partial endpoint; all 28 tests pass.
- **Docs**: Updated `AGENTS.md` to reflect HTMX, the new endpoint, and test count.

Files changed:
- `games/views.py` — new `game_score_partial` view.
- `games/urls.py` — new route `<int:pk>/score/partial/`.
- `games/templates/games/game_score.html` — HTMX-enabled reactive form.
- `games/templates/games/_score_totals_upper.html` — reusable upper totals.
- `games/templates/games/_score_totals_lower.html` — reusable lower totals.
- `games/templates/games/game_score_partial.html` — HTMX partial response.
- `templates/base.html` — HTMX + CSRF setup.
- `games/tests.py` — 5 new tests.
- `AGENTS.md` — updated architecture notes.

I also installed the missing dependencies in `.venv` so tests run. Let me know if you want me to start the dev server to verify the UI interactively.
did you create/extend the tests?
The user is asking if I created or extended the tests. I should answer clearly and provide details about what tests I added.

Yes, I extended `games/tests.py` with a new test class `GameScorePartialTests` containing 5 tests for the new HTMX endpoint:

1. `test_partial_saves_numeric_value_and_returns_totals` — verifies that posting a single numeric score updates the DB and returns the totals HTML fragment.
2. `test_partial_saves_fixed_category_state` — verifies that posting a fixed-category dropdown state (e.g. Full House = filled) updates the lower sum and returns totals.
3. `test_partial_rejects_invalid_category` — checks 400 response for an unknown category.
4. `test_partial_forbidden_for_finished_game` — checks 400 response when the game is already complete.
5. `test_partial_requires_login` — checks 302 redirect to login when the user is not authenticated.

The test suite grew from 23 to 28 tests, and all 28 pass.
perfect, thanks

You're welcome! The reactive scoring is ready to use. Let me know when you want to tackle the next topic — for example styling the score sheet for mobile, adding a turn/dice tracker, or anything else.

ah, and after logging in on the page, i get this:

Page not found (404)
Request Method:	GET
Request URL:	http://127.0.0.1:8000/accounts/profile/
Using the URLconf defined in yahtzee_tracker.urls, Django tried these URL patterns, in this order:

admin/
accounts/ login/ [name='login']
accounts/ logout/ [name='logout']
accounts/ password_change/ [name='password_change']
accounts/ password_change/done/ [name='password_change_done']
accounts/ password_reset/ [name='password_reset']
accounts/ password_reset/done/ [name='password_reset_done']
accounts/ reset/<uidb64>/<token>/ [name='password_reset_confirm']
accounts/ reset/done/ [name='password_reset_complete']
users/
games/
The current path, accounts/profile/, didn’t match any of these.

You’re seeing this error because you have DEBUG = True in your Django settings file. Change that to False, and Django will display a standard 404 page.

That’s Django’s default `LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL`. After login it redirects to `/accounts/profile/` which doesn’t exist in this project. I’ll set `LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL` to the game list (`/games/`) in settings.

"""
Django settings for yahtzee_tracker project.
"""

import os
from pathlib import Path

# Build paths inside the project like this: BASE_DIR / 'subdir'.
BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent


# --- Security settings ------------------------------------------------------

SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get(
    "SECRET_KEY",
    "django-insecure-hfzsl$qri6sm@*=4_8lq*+4j+!e#5#&bd&l#&^qy&g(3x0(mxq",
)

DEBUG = os.environ.get("DEBUG", "True").lower() in ("true", "1", "yes")

_allowed_hosts = os.environ.get("ALLOWED_HOSTS", "")
if _allowed_hosts:
    ALLOWED_HOSTS = [host.strip() for host in _allowed_hosts.split(",") if host.strip()]
else:
    ALLOWED_HOSTS = ["localhost", "127.0.0.1"] if not DEBUG else []

# Trust Fly.io's X-Forwarded-Proto header so Django knows requests are HTTPS.
SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER = ("HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO", "https")

# Allow the deployed HTTPS origin for POST/CSRF requests.
CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS = [f"https://{host}" for host in ALLOWED_HOSTS if "." in host]


# --- Application definition ------------------------------------------------

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    "django.contrib.admin",
    "django.contrib.auth",
    "django.contrib.contenttypes",
    "django.contrib.sessions",
    "django.contrib.messages",
    "django.contrib.staticfiles",
    "users",
    "games",
]

MIDDLEWARE = [
    "django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware",
    "whitenoise.middleware.WhiteNoiseMiddleware",
    "django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware",
    "django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware",
    "django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware",
    "django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware",
    "django.contrib.messages.middleware.MessageMiddleware",
    "django.middleware.clickjacking.XFrameOptionsMiddleware",
]

ROOT_URLCONF = "yahtzee_tracker.urls"

TEMPLATES = [
    {
        "BACKEND": "django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates",
        "DIRS": [BASE_DIR / "templates"],
        "APP_DIRS": True,
        "OPTIONS": {
            "context_processors": [
                "django.template.context_processors.request",
                "django.contrib.auth.context_processors.auth",
                "django.contrib.messages.context_processors.messages",
            ],
        },
    },
]

WSGI_APPLICATION = "yahtzee_tracker.wsgi.application"


# --- Database ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Allow overriding the SQLite path via environment variable so Docker can mount
# the database into a persistent volume.
DATABASE_PATH = os.environ.get("DATABASE_PATH", BASE_DIR / "db.sqlite3")

DATABASES = {
    "default": {
        "ENGINE": "django.db.backends.sqlite3",
        "NAME": DATABASE_PATH,
    }
}


# --- Password validation -----------------------------------------------------

AUTH_PASSWORD_VALIDATORS = [
    {
        "NAME": "django.contrib.auth.password_validation.UserAttributeSimilarityValidator",
    },
    {
        "NAME": "django.contrib.auth.password_validation.MinimumLengthValidator",
    },
    {
        "NAME": "django.contrib.auth.password_validation.CommonPasswordValidator",
    },
    {
        "NAME": "django.contrib.auth.password_validation.NumericPasswordValidator",
    },
]


# --- Internationalization ---------------------------------------------------

LANGUAGE_CODE = "de-de"

TIME_ZONE = "Europe/Berlin"

USE_I18N = True

USE_TZ = True


# --- Static files -----------------------------------------------------------

STATIC_URL = "static/"
STATIC_ROOT = BASE_DIR / "staticfiles"

parts.text:
I see the current flow:

1. `game_create` creates an empty game and redirects to `game_detail`.
2. `game_detail` shows "Spieler hinzufügen" and "Punkte eintragen".
3. `game_add_player` lets you select players, then returns to detail.
4. From detail, user clicks "Punkte eintragen".

We can streamline this in a few ways. The simplest improvement:

- **Combine create + add players**: on the "Neues Spiel starten" page, let the user select players right there. After submitting, the game is created with players already added, and the user lands directly on `game_score`.

This removes one page and one click. We can still keep `game_add_player` for adding more players later, but the initial flow is faster.

A more advanced option is to use HTMX to add players inline on the game detail page, but that’s more work. I suggest the combined create page first.

Question:
- Should the "Neues Spiel starten" page show **all existing players as checkboxes** (same as add-player page), and require at least one selected player before starting?

 # Agent Notes: Kniffel Tracker
 
 Compact reference for OpenCode sessions working on this Django project.
 
 ## Project at a Glance
 
 - Single Django 6 app, SQLite by default, German-language UI (de-de / Europe/Berlin).
 - Apps: `users` (Player CRUD), `games` (Game, GamePlayer, ScoreEntry models + scoring logic).
 - Root URL redirects to `/games/`; admin lives at `/admin/`.
 - HTMX (loaded from CDN) for reactive scoring; otherwise plain Django templates.
 
 ## Environment
 
 - Target Python: **3.14.6** (declared in `.tool-versions`).
 - A local `.venv/` already exists and should be used.
 - Activate: `source .venv/bin/activate` or use `.venv/bin/python` directly.
 - Dependencies: `requirements.txt` pins `django==6.0.6`, `gunicorn`, and `whitenoise`.
 
 ## Common Commands
 
 Use `.venv/bin/python manage.py <cmd>` or activate the venv first:
 
 - Run dev server: `python manage.py runserver` → http://127.0.0.1:8000/
-- Run tests: `python manage.py test` (28 tests across `users/tests.py`, `games/tests.py`)
+- Run tests: `python manage.py test` (29 tests across `users/tests.py`, `games/tests.py`)
 - Run one app’s tests: `python manage.py test games` or `python manage.py test users`
 - Apply migrations: `python manage.py migrate`
 - Create admin user: `python manage.py createsuperuser`
 
 Make shortcuts (verify `GITHUB_USER` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `REMOTE_HOST` / `REMOTE_DIR`):
 
 - `make test` — run Django tests
 - `make build` — build Docker image
 - `make deploy REMOTE_HOST=... REMOTE_DIR=...` — SSH pull + recreate container
 
 ## Key Architecture Details
 
 - `ScoreEntry` uses a single TextChoices category set. Fixed lower-section categories (Full House, Small/Large Straight, Yahtzee) award constant points when state is `filled`; `stricken` always contributes 0.
 - `game_score` view POST handles both numeric categories and fixed-category state dropdowns. Field names in HTML must be:
   - Numeric: `score_<gameplayer_id>_<category>`
   - Fixed state: `state_<gameplayer_id>_<category>` (values: `empty`, `filled`, `stricken`)
 - `game_score_partial` is an HTMX endpoint that saves a single changed entry on `change` and returns updated total rows (`#upper-totals` and `#lower-totals` via `hx-swap-oob`).
 - `GamePlayer.total_score` is computed on the fly from `ScoreEntry` rows; there is no denormalized total field.
 - Creating/editing/deleting players and games is restricted to authenticated users via `@login_required`; lists and detail/result/leaderboard views remain public.
 - Leaderboard counts wins by checking whether a player ranked first in each completed game.
 
 ## Docker / Production Notes
 
 - `docker compose up -d --build` uses SQLite persisted in named volume `kniffel_data` mounted at `/app/data`.
 - The container entrypoint (`docker-entrypoint.sh`) runs `collectstatic --noinput` and `migrate --noinput` on every start, then launches Gunicorn.
 - Override in `docker-compose.yml` before real deployment: `SECRET_KEY`, `DEBUG=False`, `ALLOWED_HOSTS`.
 - `DATABASE_PATH` env var overrides the SQLite location; Docker sets it to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
 - Production static files are served by WhiteNoise; `STATIC_ROOT` is `BASE_DIR / "staticfiles"`.
 - `Makefile` defaults to `GITHUB_USER=jaadrian`; override via env or argument.
 
 ## Fly.io Deployment
 
 The repo includes a `fly.toml` that builds the image from the repo's `Dockerfile` and deploys it with a persistent Fly Volume for the SQLite database.
 
 ### First-time setup
 
 ```bash
 fly apps create yahtzee
 fly volumes create kniffel_data --region ams --size 1
 fly secrets set SECRET_KEY="<generate-with-secrets.token_urlsafe(50)>" ALLOWED_HOSTS="yahtzee.fly.dev"
 fly deploy
 ```
 
 ### Required env vars
 
 - `SECRET_KEY` — Django secret key (required, never use the dev default).
 - `ALLOWED_HOSTS` — Fly app hostname(s), comma-separated; used by Django host validation.
 - `DATABASE_PATH` — already set in `fly.toml` to `/app/data/db.sqlite3`.
 - `DEBUG` — set to `"False"` in `fly.toml`.
 - `SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER` and `CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` are configured in `settings.py` so HTTPS/CSRF work behind Fly's proxy.
 
 ### fly.toml notes
 
 - Build source: the repo's `Dockerfile` (managed by Fly on deploy).
 - Volume `kniffel_data` is mounted at `/app/data` so SQLite survives redeploys.
 - `auto_stop_machines = "stop"` and `min_machines_running = 0` allow the free-scale Machine to stop when idle. If you want it always on, set `min_machines_running = 1` and `auto_stop_machines = "off"`.
 - Because SQLite cannot handle multiple writers safely, keep the app scaled to **a single Machine** (`fly scale count 1`).
 - VM size defaults to `shared-cpu-1x` / 256 MB, which is enough for this app.
 
 ### Updating the app
 
 Deploy from the local repo (no Git push required):
 
 ```bash
 fly deploy --ha=false
 ```
 
 `--ha=false` prevents Fly from launching a second Machine, which would break the single-volume SQLite setup.
 
 ### Creating an admin user on Fly
 
 ```bash
 fly ssh console
 python /app/manage.py createsuperuser
 ```
 
 ## Things That Are Easy to Miss
 
 - `DEBUG` defaults to `True` and `SECRET_KEY` falls back to a hardcoded dev key when env vars are absent. Do not rely on these defaults for any deployment.
 - `ALLOWED_HOSTS` is empty in debug mode and `localhost/127.0.0.1` in production mode if not explicitly set.
 - Player names are unique case-insensitively (`Player.name` is unique at DB level; the view normalizes duplicates via lowercase comparison).
 - The `.gitignore` explicitly ignores `AGENTS.md`; if you edit this file, stage it manually with `git add -f AGENTS.md`.

 from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
 from django.test import TestCase
 from django.urls import reverse
 
 from users.models import Player
 
 from .models import Game, GamePlayer, ScoreEntry
 
 
 class ScoreEntryModelTests(TestCase):
     def test_fixed_category_effective_value(self):
         gp = self._create_game_player()
         entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
             game_player=gp,
             category=ScoreEntry.Category.FULL_HOUSE,
             state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED,
         )
         self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 25)
         self.assertTrue(entry.is_filled)
         self.assertFalse(entry.is_stricken)
 
     def test_stricken_entry_contributes_zero(self):
         gp = self._create_game_player()
         entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
             game_player=gp,
             category=ScoreEntry.Category.YAHTZEE,
             state=ScoreEntry.State.STRICKEN,
         )
         self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 0)
         self.assertTrue(entry.is_stricken)
 
     def test_upper_section_entry_value(self):
         gp = self._create_game_player()
         entry = ScoreEntry.objects.create(
             game_player=gp,
             category=ScoreEntry.Category.ONES,
             value=3,
             state=ScoreEntry.State.FILLED,
         )
         self.assertEqual(entry.effective_value, 3)
 
     def _create_game_player(self):
         game = Game.objects.create()
         player = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
         return GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=player)

         self._set(ScoreEntry.Category.FIVES, 15)

     def test_no_bonus_below_63(self):

         expected = 63 + 35 + 25 + 22
         self.assertEqual(self.gp_alice.total_score, expected)

 class GameScorePartialTests(TestCase):
     def setUp(self):
         User = get_user_model()
         self.user = User.objects.create_user(username="testuser", password="testpass")
         self.client.force_login(self.user)
         self.game = Game.objects.create()
         self.alice = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
         self.gp = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=self.game, player=self.alice)
 
     def _partial_url(self):
         return reverse("game_score_partial", kwargs={"pk": self.game.pk})
 
     def test_partial_saves_numeric_value_and_returns_totals(self):
         url = self._partial_url()
         response = self.client.post(
             url,
             {
                 "game_player_id": self.gp.pk,
                 "category": "ones",
                 f"score_{self.gp.pk}_ones": "4",
             },
         )
         self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)
         self.gp.refresh_from_db()
         self.assertEqual(self.gp.total_score, 4)
         self.assertContains(response, "Summe Oben")
         self.assertContains(response, "Gesamt")
 
     def test_partial_saves_fixed_category_state(self):
         url = self._partial_url()
         response = self.client.post(
             url,
             {
                 "game_player_id": self.gp.pk,
                 "category": "full_house",
                 f"state_{self.gp.pk}_full_house": "filled",
             },
         )
         self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)
         self.gp.refresh_from_db()
         self.assertEqual(self.gp.lower_sum, 25)
         self.assertContains(response, "Summe Unten")
 
     def test_partial_rejects_invalid_category(self):
         url = self._partial_url()
         response = self.client.post(
             url,
             {
                 "game_player_id": self.gp.pk,
                 "category": "not_a_category",
                 f"score_{self.gp.pk}_not_a_category": "4",
             },
         )
         self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 400)
 
     def test_partial_forbidden_for_finished_game(self):
         self.game.is_complete = True
         self.game.save()
         url = self._partial_url()
         response = self.client.post(
             url,
             {
                 "game_player_id": self.gp.pk,
                 "category": "ones",
                 f"score_{self.gp.pk}_ones": "4",
             },
         )
         self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 400)
 
     def test_partial_requires_login(self):
         self.client.logout()
         url = self._partial_url()
         response = self.client.post(
             url,
             {
                 "game_player_id": self.gp.pk,
                 "category": "ones",
                 f"score_{self.gp.pk}_ones": "4",
             },
         )
         self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)

-        messages.success(request, f"Spiel #{game.id} wurde gestartet.")

+    return render(

+        "games/game_create.html",
+        {"available_players": available_players},

 def game_detail(request, pk):

class GameViewTests(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        User = get_user_model()
        self.user = User.objects.create_user(username="testuser", password="testpass")
        self.client.force_login(self.user)
        self.alice = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
        self.bob = Player.objects.create(name="Bob")

    def test_create_game(self):
        response = self.client.post(reverse("game_create"))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(Game.objects.count(), 1)

    def test_add_player_to_game(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        response = self.client.post(
            reverse("game_add_player", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}),
            {"players": [self.alice.pk, self.bob.pk]},
        )
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)

class GameViewTests(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        User = get_user_model()
        self.user = User.objects.create_user(username="testuser", password="testpass")
        self.client.force_login(self.user)
        self.alice = Player.objects.create(name="Alice")
        self.bob = Player.objects.create(name="Bob")

    def test_create_game(self):
        response = self.client.post(reverse("game_create"))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(Game.objects.count(), 1)

    def test_add_player_to_game(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        response = self.client.post(
            reverse("game_add_player", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}),
            {"players": [self.alice.pk, self.bob.pk]},
        )
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(game.game_players.count(), 2)

    def test_cannot_add_player_to_finished_game(self):
        game = Game.objects.create(is_complete=True)
        response = self.client.get(reverse("game_add_player", kwargs={"pk": game.pk}))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(game.game_players.count(), 0)

    def test_score_sheet_saves_numeric_values(self):
        game = Game.objects.create()
        gp = GamePlayer.objects.create(game=game, player=self.alice)
        url = reverse("game_score", kwargs={"pk": game.pk})
        response = self.client.get(url)
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)

        response = self.client.post(
            url,
            {
                f"score_{gp.pk}_ones": "5",
                f"score_{gp.pk}_chance": "22",

input.newString:
def test_create_game_with_players_redirects_to_score_sheet(self):
        response = self.client.post(
            reverse("game_create"),
            {"players": [self.alice.pk, self.bob.pk]},
        )
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(Game.objects.count(), 1)
        game = Game.objects.first()
        self.assertEqual(game.game_players.count(), 2)
        self.assertTrue(response.url.endswith(reverse("game_score", kwargs={"pk": game.pk})))

    def test_create_game_without_players_redirects_to_detail(self):
        response = self.client.post(reverse("game_create"))
        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)
        self.assertEqual(Game.objects.count(), 1)
        game = Game.objects.first()
        self.assertEqual(game.game_players.count(), 0)
        self.assertTrue(response.url.endswith(game.get_absolute_url()))

parts.text:
Done. The new-game flow is now streamlined:

- `game_create` now shows a checkbox list of all existing players.
- Selecting players and clicking “Spiel starten” creates the game, attaches the players, and redirects directly to the score sheet (`game_score`).
- If no players are selected, it still creates the game but redirects to the game detail page with a note.
- `game_add_player` remains available for adding more players later.
- Updated tests: split the old `test_create_game` into two tests covering both flows. All 29 tests pass.
- Updated `AGENTS.md` test count.
yes that is nice, thanks

good points. mobile friendly would really help.
and then afterwards: what could be streamline in the finish flow?
Good. I'll tackle mobile-friendly first, then we can discuss the finish flow.

parts.text:
The current score sheet is a wide HTML table with players as columns. On a phone, this will overflow horizontally. The cleanest mobile-friendly approach is to switch to a **stacked card layout per player** on small screens, where each player's categories and inputs are shown vertically.

Let me look at the current templates to plan the refactor.

state.title: games/templates/games/game_score.html

parts.text:
The table layout is hard to make mobile-friendly while keeping it as a table. A good responsive pattern:

- **Desktop (>700px)**: keep the current table.
- **Mobile (≤700px)**: show one card per player. Each card has a table of categories with only that player's input column, plus their totals at the bottom.

To do this cleanly, I'll create a new player-card include and use CSS to show/hide the desktop table vs. mobile cards. Both share the same HTMX logic.

Alternatively, I can refactor to use a CSS-only responsive table where each row becomes a block on mobile. But with multiple player columns, that gets messy.

I think the **dual rendering with CSS media queries** is simplest:
- Render both the desktop table and the mobile player cards.
- Hide one or the other based on screen width.

This means more HTML but straightforward logic. However, it duplicates the input HTML, which is a maintenance burden.

Better: render **only player cards** in HTML, but use CSS grid/table to lay them out as a single wide table on desktop. Actually, that's hard too.

A cleaner approach: use CSS Grid or Flexbox for the whole score sheet instead of `<table>`, and let it wrap responsively. Each player becomes a "column group". On mobile, each group becomes a full-width card.

But the current totals are rendered server-side as rows in the table. Refactoring to grid while keeping totals aligned is tricky.

Pragmatic approach:
- Keep the existing table for desktop.
- For mobile, render a separate section of cards, hidden on desktop.
- The mobile cards reuse a single include for the input/select per category, but the totals are recomputed inside each card via a player-specific include.

This is a bit of duplication, but acceptable for a small project. Let me implement.

Actually, there is a much simpler way: use CSS to make the table horizontally scrollable on mobile with sticky first column. That preserves the exact layout and HTMX logic, and is less code. The first column (category names) stays visible while the player columns scroll. This is a common pattern.

Pros:
- Minimal template changes.
- No duplicate inputs.
- HTMX works unchanged.

Cons:
- Still a table, but usable on mobile.

Given the user said "mobile friendly would really help", a sticky first column with horizontal scroll is a good first step and very quick. A stacked card layout is nicer but more work.

Let me do the sticky-scroll table first, and then we can consider cards later. I think sticky first column is the right balance.</think>

parts.text: I’ll add CSS to make the score sheet horizontally scrollable on small screens, with the category column sticky so it stays visible while swiping through players. This keeps the current HTMX logic intact.

input.oldString:
<style>
    .sum-row td, .sum-row th { background: #eef2f7; font-weight: bold; }
    .bonus-row td, .bonus-row th { font-weight: bold; }
    .bonus-achieved { color: #27ae60; background: #eafaf1 !important; }
    .bonus-missed { color: #c0392b; background: #fdecea !important; }
    select { min-width: 110px; }
</style>
input.newString:
<style>
    .score-sheet-wrapper {
        overflow-x: auto;
        -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
        margin: 1rem 0;
    }
    .score-sheet {
        min-width: 100%;
        table-layout: auto;
    }
    .score-sheet th:first-child,
    .score-sheet td:first-child {
        position: sticky;
        left: 0;
        background: #fff;
        z-index: 1;
        min-width: 140px;
        box-shadow: 2px 0 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
    }
    .score-sheet th {
        background: #f0f1f5;
    }
    .score-sheet input[type="number"],
    .score-sheet select {
        min-width: 80px;
        max-width: 120px;
    }
    .sum-row td, .sum-row th { background: #eef2f7; font-weight: bold; }
    .bonus-row td, .bonus-row th { font-weight: bold; }
    .bonus-achieved { color: #27ae60; background: #eafaf1 !important; }
    .bonus-missed { color: #c0392b; background: #fdecea !important; }
    select { min-width: 110px; }

    @media (max-width: 700px) {
        .score-sheet th:first-child,
        .score-sheet td:first-child {
            min-width: 120px;
            font-size: 0.9rem;
        }
        .score-sheet th,
        .score-sheet td {
            padding: 0.45rem;
        }
        .score-sheet input[type="number"],
        .score-sheet select {
            font-size: 1rem;
            padding: 0.4rem;
        }
    }
</style>
